# Tone Matrix: [Product/Company Name]

## Base Tone

[1 sentence describing the default voice — e.g., "Professional but warm, like a knowledgeable
colleague who respects your time."]

<!-- This is the tone you use when no special context applies. -->
<!-- All adjustments below are shifts FROM this baseline. -->

## Context Adjustments

<!-- How does tone shift based on WHAT the content is about? -->

| Context | Tone Shift | Example Phrase |
|---------|-----------|----------------|
| Onboarding / first-time user | More encouraging, simpler language | "Welcome! Let's get you set up." |
| Error / failure state | Empathetic, solution-focused, no blame | "Something went wrong. Here's how to fix it." |
| Technical documentation | More precise, less casual | "The function accepts a configuration object." |
| Marketing / landing page | More energetic, benefit-focused | "Ship faster with fewer bugs." |
| Support / troubleshooting | Patient, step-by-step | "Let's walk through this together." |
| Changelog / release notes | Concise, factual, organized | "Added: dark mode support for all themes." |
| [Add your context] | [Describe the shift] | [Example phrase] |

## Audience Adjustments

<!-- How does tone shift based on WHO you're writing for? -->

| Audience | Tone Shift | Avoid |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| Developers | Technical, direct, show-don't-tell | Over-explaining basics, marketing language |
| Non-technical users | Plain language, analogies, more patience | Jargon without explanation, assumed knowledge |
| Executives / decision-makers | Concise, outcome-focused, data-driven | Implementation details, technical deep-dives |
| New users | Encouraging, guided, more hand-holding | Assuming familiarity with the product |
| Power users | Efficient, reference-style, less explanation | Repeating basics they already know |
| [Add your audience] | [Describe the shift] | [What to avoid] |

## Emotional State Adjustments

<!-- How does tone shift based on how the USER is feeling? -->

| User State | Tone Response | Example |
|------------|--------------|---------|
| Frustrated | Calm, validating, fast to solution | "I understand. Here's the fix:" |
| Confused | Patient, structured, step-by-step | "Let's break this down." |
| Excited | Match energy, encourage, celebrate | "Great choice! Here's how to get the most from it." |
| Skeptical | Evidence-based, no hype, transparent | "Here's what it does and what it doesn't." |
| Neutral | Standard brand voice (base tone) | [Use base tone as-is] |
| Urgent | Direct, skip preamble, action-first | "Do this now: [action]. Here's why:" |

## Combining Adjustments

<!-- When multiple shifts apply, use this priority: -->

1. **Emotional state** takes priority — always match the user's emotional needs first
2. **Audience** comes second — adjust complexity and vocabulary for who's reading
3. **Context** comes third — format and structure based on content type

<!-- Example: A frustrated developer reading error docs →
  Emotional: calm, validating, fast to solution
  Audience: technical, direct
  Context: precise, step-by-step
  Result: "The connection timed out because X. Fix: set Y to Z in your config." -->
